attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a chilled slice of potato in a sink, leaving the slicing knife in the fridge.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
### Scene overview (kitchen counter with sink and nearby fridge)
The images show a countertop section with a **stainless-steel sink** set into a light-colored counter, wood cabinets below, a **black refrigerator** immediately adjacent to the sink area, and a tiled floor/backsplash. No robot/agent is visible; per task setup, the agent is initially **not at any object’s location**.

---

## 1. Objects and their domain types

### Receptacles (from the domain)
- **Sink (1)**: a built-in stainless-steel basin with a faucet and two handles.
- **Fridge (1)**: a black, two-door style refrigerator (seen from the front).  
  - The fridge is a **receptacle** in the domain sense, but the images do **not** show its interior or door state.

*(No microwave is visible in these views.)*

### Knife-type objects
- **No knife is clearly visible.**  
  - A **metal fork** is visible in the sink basin, but it is **not** a knife (and the domain only treats “knife” specially for slicing).

### Other objects (domain “object”, not special types here)
- **Tomato (1)**: red, round tomato located **inside the sink basin**.
- **Fork (1)**: metal fork located **inside the sink basin**, to the left of the tomato.
- **Potato (1)**: brown, oval potato sitting **on the countertop to the right of the sink**.
- **Bread/roll (1)**: a brown loaf/roll-like item on the countertop near the sink area (visible at the left edge in one view).

---

## 2. Quantities by relevant type
- **sink (receptacle)**: 1
- **fridge (receptacle)**: 1
- **microwave (receptacle)**: 0 visible
- **knife (knife)**: 0 visible/identifiable
- **potato (object)**: 1
- **tomato (object)**: 1
- **fork (object)**: 1
- **bread/roll (object)**: 1

---

## 3. Spatial relationships (matching domain predicates)

### Objects in the sink (inReceptacle relationships)
- The **tomato** is **in the sink** (it is resting in the basin).
- The **fork** is **in the sink** (resting in the basin, left side).

### Objects on the counter (not in a receptacle)
- The **potato** is **on the countertop** to the **right of the sink**, not inside any receptacle.
- The **bread/roll** is on the countertop (left side of the visible counter area), not inside any receptacle.

### Fridge relations
- The **fridge** is immediately adjacent to the sink/counter area (front face visible).  
- No objects are visibly inside the fridge (its interior is not shown), so no definite **inReceptacle(x, fridge)** can be confirmed from the images.

---

## 4. Current state of each object relevant to the task

### Potato (task-relevant)
- The potato on the counter appears **whole** (no visible slices) → **not sliced** in the current scene.
- It is not shown inside the fridge → **not visibly cooled/chilled**.
- It is not being held by an agent (no agent visible).

### Knife (task-relevant)
- A slicing knife is **not visible**, so its location/state (e.g., in fridge, held, etc.) cannot be directly confirmed from these images.

### Sink contents (context)
- The sink currently contains a **tomato** and a **fork**; the sink is otherwise empty.

### Open/closed states
- The **fridge door state** is not determinable (only exterior shown; no open interior view).
- The sink is an open basin (no “open/close” state applicable in typical scenes).

---

## 5. Visual properties to distinguish items
- **Sink**: rectangular stainless basin, centered under a faucet with two round handles.
- **Tomato**: bright red sphere with a green star-shaped top, located near the center of the sink basin.
- **Fork**: silver utensil in the sink basin, left of the tomato.
- **Potato**: medium brown oval, on the counter to the right of the sink.
- **Fridge**: matte black front with curved handles; positioned just right of the sink cabinetry/counter boundary.
- **Bread/roll**: brown rounded loaf/roll at the far left edge of the counter in one view.

---

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
(define (problem put_task_kitchen_scene_01)
  (:domain put_task)

  (:objects
    robot - agent

    kitchen_sink - sink
    kitchen_fridge - fridge

    potato1 - object
    tomato1 - object
    fork1 - object
    bread1 - object

    slicing_knife1 - knife
  )

  (:init
    ;; receptacle properties
    (openable kitchen_fridge)

    ;; initial object placements from the scene
    (inReceptacle tomato1 kitchen_sink)
    (inReceptacle fork1 kitchen_sink)

    ;; potato and bread are on the counter (modeled as not in any receptacle)
    ;; (no inReceptacle facts for potato1 or bread1)

    ;; knife required by the instruction; place it in the fridge initially
    (inReceptacle slicing_knife1 kitchen_fridge)

    ;; robot starts not at any object's location (so no atLocation facts for robot)
  )

  (:goal
    (and
      ;; "Put a chilled slice of potato in a sink"
      (isSliced potato1)
      (isCool potato1)
      (inReceptacle potato1 kitchen_sink)

      ;; "leaving the slicing knife in the fridge"
      (inReceptacle slicing_knife1 kitchen_fridge)
    )
  )
)

)
attempts.plan:
(gotolocation robot kitchen_fridge)
(pickupobject robot slicing_knife1)
(gotolocation robot potato1)
(sliceobject robot potato1 slicing_knife1)
(gotolocation robot kitchen_fridge)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot slicing_knife1 kitchen_fridge)
(gotolocation robot potato1)
(pickupobject robot potato1)
(gotolocation robot kitchen_fridge)
(coolobject robot kitchen_fridge potato1)
(gotolocation robot kitchen_sink)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot potato1 kitchen_sink)
